title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. obtusa
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. obtusa is a subspecies of the lady's mantle plant, a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. obtusa are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and wound-healing properties, making it useful for treating menstrual disorders, digestive issues, and skin problems. Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. obtusa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various conditions.
